title: Earnings Manipulation: If a firm has an EBIT of $1,000,000, interest expenses...
summary: Calculate net income from EBIT by subtracting interest expense and applying the tax rate.
prompt: If a firm has an EBIT of $1,000,000, interest expenses of $250,000, and a tax rate of 25%, what is the net income before extraordinary items?
solution: Net Income = (EBIT - Interest Expense) × (1 - Tax Rate) = ($1,000,000 - $250,000) × (1 - 0.25) = $750,000 × 0.75 = $562,500. This net income figure is crucial for evaluating the firm’s profitability after accounting for expenses and taxes.
